steps.title: Simplify the given equation
steps.content: Start with ${13}(x-n)=13y+13n$. Expanding gives $13x-13n=13y+13n$, so $13x-13y=26n$. Divide by $13$ to get $$x-y=2n.$$

steps.title: Know the condition for no solution
steps.content: A two-line system has no solution when the lines are parallel and distinct. So the second equation should have the same left side pattern as $x-y=2n$ but a different constant.

steps.title: Compare the choices
steps.content:
The correct answer is
$$2x-2y=2n$$
Divide by $2$:
$$x-y=n$$
This is parallel to $x-y=2n$ but not the same line because $n$ is positive.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: $2x - 2y = 4n$ simplifies to the same line as the original, so it gives infinitely many solutions. The equations with $x+y$ have slope $-1$ after simplification, so they cross the original. $2x - 2y = 2n$ is parallel but separate, so there is no solution.
